characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Plot Pants: The Dojo kids normally wear a basic set of clothing, switching to power-up gear (complete with helmets and padding) to do their deliveries or fight Bedlam. However, in one episode they decide togo clubbingand switch to civilian outfits (which are, in fact,Palette Swapsof their regular models —not one polygon has changed). Artificial Human: Ol' Skool created him from the instructions of an ancient alien artifact. The Baby of the Bunch: By virtue of being a boy whoReally Was Born Yesterdayamong a group of teenagers. Badass Adorable: He's a young delivery boy withbig, blue eyesand a small stature... capable of taking on multiple evil robots and clones. Character Development: He goes from being completely clueless and naive to being sure of himself and learning to take risks.

characters.name: Dojo Deliveries
characters.description: Plot Pants: The Dojo kids normally wear a basic set of clothing, switching to power-up gear (complete with helmets and padding) to do their deliveries or fight Bedlam. However, in one episode they decide togo clubbingand switch to civilian outfits (which are, in fact,Palette Swapsof their regular models —not one polygon has changed). Artificial Human: Ol' Skool created him from the instructions of an ancient alien artifact. The Baby of the Bunch: By virtue of being a boy whoReally Was Born Yesterdayamong a group of teenagers. Badass Adorable: He's a young delivery boy withbig, blue eyesand a small stature... capable of taking on multiple evil robots and clones. Character Development: He goes from being completely clueless and naive to being sure of himself and learning to take risks.

characters.name: Dojo friends and allies
characters.description: Non-Human Sidekick: To Ed. What Happened to the Mouse?: Strangely, he is completely absent in the finale. Put on a Bus: Happens at least twice. After his first appearance, he is reprogrammed back to Bedlam's side. He reappears as "Static Part 2", where he is one of the Riot Bots that attacked the kids, and is reprogrammed back to their side. After that, he reappears in the series finale, "Ex-Machina Part 2", where it's revealed that Dr. Hong held onto him. Heel–Face Revolving Door: Throughout the series, he gets reprogrammed by Dojo, gets reprogrammed back to Bedlam's team, disappears for a while, get rescued by Dojo, and reprogrammed back to their side, disappears once more, then reappears in the finale, only to get destroyed, and later, rebuilt. Set a Mook to Kill a Mook: He is hacked by Fizz and converted to the Dojo's side. Even after being turned back to Bedlam's side, they still manage to easily convert him.

characters.name: Villains
characters.description: Bad Boss: Regularly abuses and berates his minions. Big Bad: He's responsible for most of the awful things that happen in the show and has a personal grudge against Dojo Deliveries. Corrupt Corporate Executive: He's killed rival business owners in order to gain control over Progress City. Kick the Dog: Often abuses the meek and pathetic Crouch. The Sociopath: Is more than willing to destroy the half of Progress City he doesn't own, despite the lives that would be lost. Not to mention his readiness to torture his enemies to get what he wants.

characters.name: Other courier crews
characters.description: Monster Clown: They show hints of this at times. Wacky Sound Effect: Their actions are often punctuated by cartoony sound effects. Invisibility Cloak: Makes use of a device that allows him and his vehicle to turn invisible.This particular device happens to be created for usage by Ed, though it is unknown how Dirk is able to use. Punny Name: His name is a play on the saying "dirt cheap". Faceless Goons: They all wear helmets that conceal their face, which resemble motorcycle helmets.

characters.name: Others
characters.description: He Who Must Not Be Seen: Never appears on-screen. The Voice: She talks about everything that goes on in Progress City. Benevolent Precursors Energy Being: Her form consists of a blueEnergy Ball, with the silhouette of a humanoid figure inside. Those Two Guys: They are almost always seen together.
